title: After 80 Years, Mathematicians Give Famed ‘Erdős Method’ an Upgrade

title: The Riemann Hypothesis – interactive explanation
description: An interactive journey from 'what is a prime number?' to a genuine understanding of the most important unsolved problem in mathematics.

title: How AI is reshaping discovery in maths and physics
description: Artificial intelligence is not replacing human intuition in these fields, but reimagining how questions are asked, explored and understood.

title: A Branch-Switch Obstruction Phenomenon in Primitive Euler Bricks: Computational Through 1 Billion
description:
I'mThe perfect cuboid problem asks whether there exists a rectangular box whose three edge
lengths, three face diagonals, and space diagonal are all integers. Although many Euler bricks
are known, no perfect cuboid has been discovered, and no impossibility …

title: Mathematicians Puzzled Over a Famous Problem for 80 Years. Now, They've Used A.I. to Identify a Clever Solution
description: In 1946, the mathematician Paul Erdős posed the unit distance problem—and suggested a winning strategy. An A.I. model has now landed on a better one. Why didn't humans get there first?

title: An OpenAI Model ‘Disproved’ a Famous Math Conjecture. This Mathematician Couldn’t Leave It Alone
description: Mathematician Will Sawin discusses his experience reviewing and refining a mathematical proof devised by OpenAI's internal model—and what that could mean for mathematics.
